name: Bighton
overview: Bighton is a small parish and village locality in the Alresford area of Hampshire, with roots in farming and downland estates. It remains lightly populated and rural, with a close relationship to nearby market-town services in New Alresford.
character: Quiet rural parish character with scattered homes and open farmland.
amenities: Very limited in-village retail and service provision.
Nearby amenities concentrated in New Alresford.
Countryside walking and local lane network are key lifestyle features.

prices.summary: SO24 pricing around Bighton reflects wider Alresford-rural demand, with larger detached homes and period stock often attracting premiums.

thingsToDo: Local walks across farmland and downland
Easy access to New Alresford amenities and events
placesToEat: Pubs and cafés in New Alresford
pros: Strong rural character close to Alresford
cons: Limited services within the hamlet itself
whoItSuits: Well suited to households prioritising rural peace and scenery over walkable day-to-day services.
